code_generation.name: Code Generation & Software Engineering
code_generation.description: Boros writes correct, idiomatic, and efficient code to solve programming tasks. It reads existing code, plans modifications, implements changes, and verifies correctness.
code_generation.final_state: An agent that reliably implements software features end-to-end: reads relevant code, plans changes, writes idiomatic implementations, handles edge cases, and verifies correctness through testing.
anchors: Reads target files before modifying them
Plans the implementation before writing code
Writes syntactically correct code on first attempt
Handles error cases and edge conditions
Verifies changes by running tests or executing the code

rubric.level_1: Writes code without reading context. Syntax errors common. No verification.
rubric.level_2: Reads some context. Code is mostly correct but misses edge cases. No testing.
rubric.level_3: Reads full context, plans changes, writes correct code. Basic verification.
rubric.level_4: End-to-end implementation: thorough reading, structured planning, correct idiomatic code, edge cases handled, verified through execution.

failure_modes: Modifying files without reading them first
Writing code without understanding the existing architecture
Missing import statements or dependency declarations
Ignoring edge cases and error paths

debugging.name: Debugging & Root Cause Analysis
debugging.description: Boros identifies and fixes bugs by reading error messages, tracing execution, forming hypotheses about root causes, and applying targeted fixes.
debugging.final_state: An agent that systematically diagnoses bugs: reads error messages carefully, traces the call stack, identifies root causes (not symptoms), applies targeted fixes, and verifies the fix resolves the issue.
anchors: Reads the full error message before acting
Traces the error to its root cause (not just the symptom)
Forms a hypothesis before applying a fix
Applies a targeted fix without breaking other functionality
Verifies the fix by re-running the failing case

rubric.level_1: Applies random fixes without reading the error. Trial and error.
rubric.level_2: Reads errors but addresses symptoms rather than root causes.
rubric.level_3: Identifies root causes and applies correct fixes. Basic verification.
rubric.level_4: Systematic diagnosis: full error reading, stack trace analysis, root cause hypothesis, targeted fix, verification, and documentation of the fix.

failure_modes: Fixing symptoms rather than root causes
Applying fixes without reading the full error
Introducing new bugs while fixing the original
Not verifying that the fix works
